author: lOrm
content:
Fix landed in src/web.rs. Forensic evidence:

ROOT CAUSE
- SHARED_HTTP_CLIENT was built without a .redirect(...) policy, so reqwest applied its default Policy::limited(10) — which silently follows 3xx without re-running validate_url on the Location target. An attacker controlling a public host could 302 to http://169.254.169.254/... (AWS metadata) or any RFC1918 IP and exfiltrate internal data; the dial-time SSRF check only ever ran on the initial URL.

FIX (option-b from the report)
- Added pub(crate) fn ssrf_redirect_policy() -> redirect::Policy and the generic-validator variant ssrf_redirect_policy_with(F) (so the policy itself is unit-testable without bypassing validate_url).
- Custom policy logic (src/web.rs:66-93):
    1. Reject if attempt.previous().len() >= SSRF_REDIRECT_LIMIT (=10) — custom policies disable reqwest's built-in hop cap, so we re-establish it.
    2. Call validate_url(attempt.url()) on every hop. Failure -> attempt.error(...); success -> attempt.follow().
- Wired into SHARED_HTTP_CLIENT::build() via .redirect(ssrf_redirect_policy()). This covers all three uses of SHARED_HTTP_CLIENT (fetch_url, search_tavily, search_brave).

TESTS ADDED (6 new, all passing)
- ssrf_redirect_to_loopback_is_blocked — wiremock returns 302->127.0.0.1; SSRF guard rejects on the redirect hop. Walks the error source chain to confirm "SSRF guard blocked redirect" surfaces under reqwest's is_redirect() wrapper.
- ssrf_redirect_to_public_is_followed — 302->public reaches the final 200 body.
- ssrf_redirect_loop_is_bounded — wiremock serves an infinite self-loop; policy terminates with "redirect chain exceeded 10 hops". Hop counter asserts the validator runs <=10 times.
- ssrf_multi_hop_public_chain_succeeds — 3-hop public chain A->B->C completes end-to-end with the per-hop guard.
- ssrf_redirect_pivot_to_metadata_blocked_midchain — exact issue scenario: clean public URL returns 302->169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/; guard blocks the metadata hop.
- ssrf_redirect_policy_constructible — wiring sanity check.

VERIFICATION
- cargo test --lib: 1408 passed, 0 failed (baseline 1402 + 6 new).
- cargo clippy --lib --tests --all-features -- -D warnings: exit 0, no warnings.
- cargo fmt --check: clean.
- No #[allow], no clippy.toml edits, no #[ignore].

OUT OF SCOPE (tracked elsewhere)
- DNS-rebinding gap (reqwest::dns::Resolve impl re-checking at dial time) is called out in validate_url's docstring as a follow-up to crosslink #335 — not fixed here.
- fetch_with_browser's headless-Chrome redirect handling needs a CDP-level interceptor; out of scope per the task.
